items.title: PCG nabs 5 for illegal fishing in Romblon town
items.snippet: CALAPAN CITY, ORIENTAL MINDORO — The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) apprehended five individuals for alleged illegal fishing using a compressor in the waters off Isabel Island in Banton town, Romblon province on March 2. items.title: China’s Pacific squid fishery rife with labor, fishing abuses: Report
items.snippet: Labor abuses including violence, debt bondage and withheld wages and medical care, overfishing, shark finning, marine mammal killings: A new report exposes bad practices and a weak regulatory framework governing the jumbo flying squid fishery in the Southeast Pacific Ocean. items.title: No individual allowed to undermine national efforts to combat IUU fishing: PM
items.snippet: Prime Minister Pham Minh Chinh has stressed that wrongdoings by any individual or organisation must not be allowed to affect the country’s overall efforts to have the European Commission’s “yellow card” warning lifted.

items.title: Acting minister urges EAEU to further open market for Vietnamese aquatic products
items.snippet: Both host and guest agreed to facilitate market access, promptly remove trade barriers and address difficulties in order to meet cooperation needs and fully tap the economic potential of the EAEU and Vietnam.

items.title: Displaced for conservation, South Africa’s Thonga try to keep a fishing tradition alive

items.title: PM chairs 32nd meeting of national steering committee for anti-IUU fishing
items.snippet: The highest objective is to meet the dual requirement of lifting the EC’s yellow-card warning and ensuring the sustainable development of the fisheries sector, PM Chinh underscored.

items.title: Khanh Hoa ensures transparency in data to address IUU fishing
items.snippet: Determined to have the EC’s “yellow card” warning lifted, Khanh Hoa has implemented drastic measures against IUU fishing in recent years.

items.title: ‘Borneo states key to climate plan’
items.snippet: KUCHING: The forests and rich biodiver­sity of Sabah and Sarawak play a central role in Malaysia's climate strategy, says Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Openg.

items.title: Myanmar elections marked by fear as UN warns civilians are coerced from all sides
items.snippet: As Myanmar approaches elections scheduled for 28 December, the UN’s top human rights official has said that civilians are being coerced from all sides – forced by the military to vote and threatened by armed opposition groups to boycott – in a climate of fear, violence and mass repression.

items.title: Pagasa: El Niño may develop August to October, intense habagat looms
items.snippet: [Updated March 3, 2026, 7:32 p.m.] MANILA, Philippines — The El Niño phenomenon may start developing by August to October this year, according to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa). items.title: Decades of gains overshadowed as Asia-Pacific falls behind on sustainable development targets
items.snippet: Climate inaction, biodiversity loss and rising emissions are pushing Asia and the Pacific further off course on the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), with the region set to miss nearly nine out of ten targets by 2030, the United Nations has warned.

items.title: America’s national parks face an uncertain future as climate risks mount
items.snippet: America’s national parks were conceived as sanctuaries from the forces remaking the rest of the continent. Climate change is now breaching that boundary. items.title: Floods, drought and insecurity drive acute hunger for 7.5 million in Pakistan
items.snippet: After a year that saw heavy monsoon floods, prolonged drought and dry spells, and a surge in violence, 7.5 million people in Pakistan face high levels of food insecurity and malnutrition according to a report from the global hunger monitor.

items.title: Climate change is slowing southern right whale birth rate, 33-year study finds
items.snippet: Southern right whales (Eubalaena australis) off Australia’s southern coast are having calves less often than they used to. items.title: Climate change drives uneven shifts in tree diversity across Amazon and Andes
items.snippet: The tropical forests of the Amazon and Andes are some of the most biodiverse places on the planet, but across both regions, changes in climate and landscape conditions are driving a shift in the number of tree species, recent research has found. items.title: Russian LNG Carrier ‘Arctic Metagaz’ Reportedly Ablaze Off Malta as Maritime Patrol Aircraft Circles
items.snippet: An LNG carrier identified as the Arctic Metagaz, part of Russia’s emerging shadow fleet transporting sanctioned liquefied natural gas, was reportedly on fire early Tuesday in the central Mediterranean off the coasts of Malta and Libya.

items.title: Vietnam Border Guard - the nation’s steel shield
items.snippet: On March 3, 1959, the People’s Armed Police Force - now the Vietnam Border Guard - was officially established. The event marked an important milestone in the strategic policy of the Party and the State regarding the protection of national sovereignty and border security.

items.title: Vietnam opposes use of force against sovereign nations: Spokesperson
items.snippet: Vietnam calls upon all parties involved to act with responsibility, strictly adhere to international law, the United Nations Charter and relevant United Nations resolutions, respect the sovereignty and territorial integrity of nations, resume negotiations, and create favourable conditions for dialogue.

items.title: Vietnamese vessels urged to adopt highest security measures in Middle East waters
